# Wiki Layer Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (if subagents available) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Ship a deterministic, rule-based per-chapter and per-work wiki layer that sits between the primary source and the LLM consumer, generated by classical statistical algorithms with zero LLM tokens in any output. Cards (~280 tokens) for cheap navigation; full sheets (~1.5K tokens) for opt-in deeper analysis. Two new MCP tools surface the artifact.

**Architecture:** A new `scripts/build-wiki.ts` orchestrator drives the pipeline: tokenize each chapter (language-aware per D5), compute statistical primitives (TF-IDF n-grams, NPMI collocations, TextRank, refrain detection, vocabulary stats), render markdown card + full sheet per chapter and per work, write atomically to `corpus/works/<slug>/wiki/`. Existing TF-IDF helpers in `scripts/cross-link.ts` get extracted to a shared `apps/mcp/lib/tfidf.ts` module so both `cross-link.ts` and the wiki build use the same IDF values. Two new MCP tools (`read_wiki`, `read_wiki_full`) load the markdown on demand.

**Tech Stack:** Bun + TypeScript. Bun's built-in `node:fs`, `node:path`, `node:crypto`. Markdown via plain string concatenation (no marked). Tests via `bun:test`. CI via GitHub Actions with `bun run scripts/build-wiki.ts --check` and `bun run scripts/bench-wiki.ts`.

**Reference design:** `docs/designs/wiki-layer-rule-based-summaries.md` (status: APPROVED, locked decisions D1–D7 + R1).

---

## Chunk 1: Foundation — shared TF-IDF + language-aware tokenizer

Extract pure functions from `scripts/cross-link.ts` into a shared library; refactor cross-link.ts to import from the shared module so cross-links and wiki agree on IDF; build a language-aware tokenizer that honors D5's rules (preserve diacritics + compound hyphens, Toronto OE Corpus stopwords).

```typescript
describe("tfidf shared module", () => {
  test("re-exports the constants used by cross-link.ts", () => {
    expect(MIN_TOKENS).toBe(50);
    expect(MIN_COSINE).toBe(0.05);
    expect(DEFAULT_TOP_K).toBe(5);

  test("tokenize splits English text on whitespace + punctuation, lowercases, drops 1-2 char tokens", () => {
    const out = tokenize("The Self-Existent Lord, undiscernible at first.");
    expect(out).toContain("self-existent"); // hyphenated compound preserved (D5)
    expect(out).toContain("undiscernible");
    expect(out).not.toContain("at"); // 2-char dropped
    expect(out).not.toContain("the"); // 3-char kept (yes, "the" stays unless stopwords)
  });

  test("buildTfIdf produces vectors per chapter id with nonempty terms", () => {
    const docs = [
      { id: "ch1", tokens: ["dharma", "creation", "self-existent"] },
      { id: "ch2", tokens: ["dharma", "verse", "stanza"] },
    ];
    const { vectors } = buildTfIdf(docs);
    expect(vectors.get("ch1")).toBeDefined();
    expect(vectors.get("ch1")!.size).toBeGreaterThan(0);
  });

  test("cosine of identical vectors is 1.0; orthogonal is 0.0", () => {
    const a = new Map([["x", 0.5], ["y", 0.5]]);
    const b = new Map([["x", 0.5], ["y", 0.5]]);
    const c = new Map([["z", 1.0]]);
    expect(cosine(a, b)).toBeCloseTo(1.0, 5);
    expect(cosine(a, c)).toBe(0);
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 1.3: Create the shared module by extracting from `scripts/cross-link.ts`**

`apps/mcp/lib/tfidf.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * Shared TF-IDF primitives. Extracted from scripts/cross-link.ts so the
 * cross-link build and the wiki layer build operate on the same IDF
 * values. Pure functions, no I/O.
 */

export const MIN_TOKENS = 50;
export const MIN_COSINE = 0.05;
export const DEFAULT_TOP_K = 5;

const TOKEN_RE = /[\s,.;:()\[\]{}<>"'!?—–]+/u;

/**
 * Default English-leaning tokenizer. Lowercases, splits on whitespace +
 * punctuation BUT NOT on hyphens (D5 — hyphenated compounds stay atomic),
 * drops 1-2 character tokens.
 */
export function tokenize(body: string): string[] {
  return body
    .toLowerCase()
    .split(TOKEN_RE)
    .filter((t) => t.length >= 3);
}

export interface TfIdfDoc {
  id: string;
  tokens: string[];
}

export interface TfIdfResult {
  vectors: Map<string, Map<string, number>>;
  idf: Map<string, number>;
  N: number;
}

function termFrequency(tokens: string[]): Map<string, number> {
  const tf = new Map<string, number>();
  for (const t of tokens) tf.set(t, (tf.get(t) ?? 0) + 1);
  return tf;
}

export function buildTfIdf(docs: TfIdfDoc[]): TfIdfResult {
  const N = docs.length;
  const df = new Map<string, number>();
  const tfPerDoc = new Map<string, Map<string, number>>();
  for (const d of docs) {
    const tf = termFrequency(d.tokens);
    tfPerDoc.set(d.id, tf);
    for (const term of tf.keys()) df.set(term, (df.get(term) ?? 0) + 1);
  }
  const idf = new Map<string, number>();
  for (const [term, dfCount] of df) {
    idf.set(term, Math.log((N + 1) / (dfCount + 1)) + 1);
  }
  const vectors = new Map<string, Map<string, number>>();
  for (const [id, tf] of tfPerDoc) {
    const total = [...tf.values()].reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
    const v = new Map<string, number>();
    for (const [term, count] of tf) {
      const tfNorm = total > 0 ? count / total : 0;
      v.set(term, tfNorm * (idf.get(term) ?? 0));
    }
    vectors.set(id, v);
  }
  return { vectors, idf, N };
}

export function cosine(a: Map<string, number>, b: Map<string, number>): number {
  let dot = 0;
  let normA = 0;
  let normB = 0;
  for (const v of a.values()) normA += v * v;
  for (const v of b.values()) normB += v * v;
  if (normA === 0 || normB === 0) return 0;
  const smaller = a.size <= b.size ? a : b;
  const larger = smaller === a ? b : a;
  for (const [k, v] of smaller) {
    const u = larger.get(k);
    if (u !== undefined) dot += v * u;
  }
  return dot / (Math.sqrt(normA) * Math.sqrt(normB));
}
```

```typescript
import { describe, expect, test } from "bun:test";
import { tokenize, type Language } from "./tokenize";

describe("language-aware tokenize (D5)", () => {
  test("English: applies English stopwords, lowercase, ≥3 char filter", () => {
    const out = tokenize("The dharma of the cosmos.", "english");
    expect(out).toContain("dharma");
    expect(out).toContain("cosmos");
    expect(out).not.toContain("the"); // English stopword
    expect(out).not.toContain("of"); // English stopword
  });

  test("Sanskrit: preserves diacritics (D5: ātmā stays one token)", () => {
    const out = tokenize("ātmā samāhitaḥ vidyā", "sanskrit");
    expect(out).toContain("ātmā");
    expect(out).toContain("samāhitaḥ");
    expect(out).toContain("vidyā");
  });

  test("Urdu: preserves hyphenated compounds as one token (D5)", () => {
    const out = tokenize("dil-e-naadan tujhe hua kya hai", "urdu");
    expect(out).toContain("dil-e-naadan");
    expect(out).not.toContain("naadan"); // not split on hyphen
  });

  test("Sanskrit: preserves hyphenated compounds (varna-dharma stays atomic)", () => {
    const out = tokenize("varna-dharma is the topic", "sanskrit");
    expect(out).toContain("varna-dharma");
  });

  test("Old English: applies Toronto OE Corpus stopwords", () => {
    // 'and' and 'on' are in the OE stopword list
    const out = tokenize("Wyrd biþ ful aræd and on heofon", "old-english");
    expect(out).toContain("wyrd");
    expect(out).toContain("heofon");
    expect(out).not.toContain("and");
  });

  test("rejects unknown languages by throwing", () => {
    expect(() => tokenize("test", "klingon" as Language)).toThrow();
  });
});
```

`apps/mcp/lib/wiki/tokenize.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * Language-aware tokenizer per design doc D5:
 *
 * - Lowercase always.
 * - Split on whitespace + brackets/parens + most punctuation, BUT NOT on
 *   hyphens (so "dil-e-naadan", "varna-dharma" stay atomic).
 * - Preserve diacritics (no NFKD normalization). "ātmā" stays one token.
 * - Drop tokens of length < 3.
 * - Apply per-language stopword list when available.
 *
 * Languages without a stopword list (sanskrit, urdu, kawi/old-javanese):
 * no stopword removal — TF-IDF handles stopword-like high-DF terms via the
 * IDF score itself.
 */

import { EN_STOPWORDS } from "./stopwords/en";
import { FR_STOPWORDS } from "./stopwords/fr";
import { DE_STOPWORDS } from "./stopwords/de";
import { OE_STOPWORDS } from "./stopwords/oe";

export type Language =
  | "english"
  | "french"
  | "german"
  | "old-english"
  | "sanskrit"
  | "urdu"
  | "kawi"
  | "latin";

const TOKEN_RE = /[\s,.;:()\[\]{}<>"'!?—–…]+/u;

const STOPWORDS_BY_LANG: Partial<Record<Language, Set<string>>> = {
  english: EN_STOPWORDS,
  french: FR_STOPWORDS,
  german: DE_STOPWORDS,
  "old-english": OE_STOPWORDS,
};

const KNOWN_LANGS: Set<Language> = new Set([
  "english", "french", "german", "old-english",
  "sanskrit", "urdu", "kawi", "latin",
]);

export function tokenize(body: string, language: Language): string[] {
  if (!KNOWN_LANGS.has(language)) {
    throw new Error(`Unknown language: ${language}`);
  }
  const stopwords = STOPWORDS_BY_LANG[language];
  return body
    .toLowerCase()
    .split(TOKEN_RE)
    .filter((t) => t.length >= 3)
    .filter((t) => !stopwords?.has(t));
}
```

- [ ] **Step 3.5: Property fuzz test for tokenization edge cases**

Add to `apps/mcp/lib/wiki/tokenize.test.ts`:

```typescript
describe("tokenize property fuzz", () => {
  test("idempotent: tokenize(tokenize(x).join(' ')) ≡ tokenize(x) modulo stopwords", () => {
    // Sanskrit text doesn't have a stopword list, so this is a clean check.
    const input = "ātmā samāhitaḥ vidyā varna-dharma puruṣa";
    const once = tokenize(input, "sanskrit");
    const twice = tokenize(once.join(" "), "sanskrit");
    expect(twice).toEqual(once);
  });

  test("RTL marks and zero-width characters do not produce empty tokens", () => {
    const input = "test‏text‍other"; // RTL mark + ZWJ
    const out = tokenize(input, "english");
    expect(out.every((t) => t.length >= 3)).toBe(true);
  });

  test("smart quotes don't get embedded in tokens", () => {
    const input = `"smartquoted"text 'apostrophed'word`;
    const out = tokenize(input, "english");
    expect(out).not.toContain('"smartquoted"');
    expect(out).not.toContain("'apostrophed'");
    expect(out).toContain("smartquoted");
  });

  test("combining marks are preserved (NFC stays NFC)", () => {
    // NFC ātmā: ā single codepoint; NFD: a + ̄ combining macron
    const nfc = "ātmā";
    const out = tokenize(nfc + " test", "sanskrit");
    expect(out[0]).toBe(nfc.toLowerCase());
  });
});
```

`apps/mcp/lib/wiki/ngrams.test.ts`:

```typescript
import { describe, expect, test } from "bun:test";
import { ngrams } from "./ngrams";

describe("ngrams", () => {
  test("unigrams equals input tokens", () => {
    expect(ngrams(["a", "b", "c"], 1)).toEqual([["a"], ["b"], ["c"]]);
  });

  test("bigrams produce N-1 pairs", () => {
    expect(ngrams(["a", "b", "c"], 2)).toEqual([["a", "b"], ["b", "c"]]);
  });

  test("trigrams produce N-2 triples", () => {
    expect(ngrams(["a", "b", "c", "d"], 3)).toEqual([
      ["a", "b", "c"],
      ["b", "c", "d"],
    ]);
  });

  test("empty input → empty output for any n", () => {
    expect(ngrams([], 2)).toEqual([]);
  });

  test("n > tokens.length → empty output", () => {
    expect(ngrams(["a"], 3)).toEqual([]);
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 4.2: Run; verify fail**

```bash
bun test apps/mcp/lib/wiki/ngrams.test.ts
```

- [ ] **Step 4.3: Implement**

`apps/mcp/lib/wiki/ngrams.ts`:

```typescript
/** Sliding-window n-gram extraction. Pure, no allocation surprises. */
export function ngrams<T>(tokens: T[], n: number): T[][] {
  if (n <= 0 || tokens.length < n) return [];
  const out: T[][] = [];
  for (let i = 0; i <= tokens.length - n; i++) {
    out.push(tokens.slice(i, i + n));
  }
  return out;
}
```

- [ ] **Step 5.1: Write failing test**

`apps/mcp/lib/wiki/npmi.test.ts`:

```typescript
import { describe, expect, test } from "bun:test";
import { computeNPMI } from "./npmi";

describe("NPMI", () => {
  test("perfect collocation (always together) gives NPMI ≈ 1.0", () => {
    // "fish spit" appears in every doc, never separately — full collocation
    const docs = [
      ["fish", "spit", "alpha"],
      ["fish", "spit", "beta"],
      ["fish", "spit", "gamma"],
    ];
    const out = computeNPMI(docs, { minJointCount: 2 });
    const fishSpit = out.find((c) => c.a === "fish" && c.b === "spit");
    expect(fishSpit).toBeDefined();
    expect(fishSpit!.npmi).toBeGreaterThan(0.9);
  });

  test("orthogonal pairs produce low NPMI", () => {
    const docs = [
      ["alpha", "beta", "gamma", "delta"],
      ["epsilon", "zeta", "eta", "theta"],
    ];
    const out = computeNPMI(docs, { minJointCount: 1 });
    // No co-occurrence at all between {alpha} and {epsilon}
    const ae = out.find((c) => c.a === "alpha" && c.b === "epsilon");
    expect(ae).toBeUndefined();
  });

  test("filter: minJointCount drops rare pairs", () => {
    const docs = [["a", "b"], ["c", "d"], ["e", "f"]];
    const out = computeNPMI(docs, { minJointCount: 3 });
    expect(out).toEqual([]);
  });

  test("returns sorted desc by NPMI", () => {
    const docs = [
      ["a", "b", "c"],
      ["a", "b", "d"],
      ["a", "b", "e"],
      ["c", "d"],
    ];
    const out = computeNPMI(docs, { minJointCount: 2 });
    for (let i = 0; i < out.length - 1; i++) {
      expect(out[i]!.npmi).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(out[i + 1]!.npmi);
    }
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 5.3: Implement**

`apps/mcp/lib/wiki/npmi.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * Normalized Pointwise Mutual Information (Bouma 2009).
 *
 * NPMI(a,b) = log(P(a,b) / (P(a) * P(b))) / -log(P(a,b))
 *
 * Range: [-1, 1]. Higher = stronger collocation. Filter on minJointCount
 * to avoid sample noise from rare co-occurrences.
 */

export interface NPMIOpts {
  minJointCount?: number;
}

export interface NPMIResult {
  a: string;
  b: string;
  npmi: number;
  joint_count: number;
}

export function computeNPMI(
  docs: string[][],
  opts: NPMIOpts = {},
): NPMIResult[] {
  const minJoint = opts.minJointCount ?? 3;
  // Count individual term occurrences (across docs, weighted by doc count
  // since this is a paragraph-level corpus).
  const termCount = new Map<string, number>();
  const pairCount = new Map<string, number>(); // key = "a\0b" (a < b)
  let totalUnigrams = 0;
  let totalPairs = 0;

  for (const doc of docs) {
    const seen = new Set<string>();
    for (const t of doc) {
      termCount.set(t, (termCount.get(t) ?? 0) + 1);
      totalUnigrams += 1;
      seen.add(t);
    }
    const sorted = [...seen].sort();
    for (let i = 0; i < sorted.length; i++) {
      for (let j = i + 1; j < sorted.length; j++) {
        const k = `${sorted[i]}\0${sorted[j]}`;
        pairCount.set(k, (pairCount.get(k) ?? 0) + 1);
        totalPairs += 1;
      }
    }
  }

  const out: NPMIResult[] = [];
  for (const [key, joint] of pairCount) {
    if (joint < minJoint) continue;
    const [a, b] = key.split("\0") as [string, string];
    const pA = (termCount.get(a) ?? 0) / totalUnigrams;
    const pB = (termCount.get(b) ?? 0) / totalUnigrams;
    const pAB = joint / totalPairs;
    if (pAB <= 0 || pA <= 0 || pB <= 0) continue;
    const pmi = Math.log(pAB / (pA * pB));
    const npmi = pmi / -Math.log(pAB);
    out.push({ a, b, npmi, joint_count: joint });
  }
  out.sort((x, y) => y.npmi - x.npmi);
  return out;
}
```

```typescript
  test("uniform matrix → uniform scores → low confidence (D3)", () => {
    const sim = Array.from({ length: 6 }, () => Array(6).fill(0.5));
    for (let i = 0; i < 6; i++) sim[i]![i] = 1.0;
    const { scores, confidence } = textRank(sim);

    expect(variance).toBeLessThan(0.01);

    ];
```

- [ ] **Step 6.3: Implement**

`apps/mcp/lib/wiki/textrank.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * TextRank-on-paragraphs (Mihalcea & Tarau 2004).
 *
 * Build similarity graph: nodes = paragraphs, edges = TF-IDF cosine.
 * Drop edges below threshold (default 0.1). Run PageRank with damping
 * 0.85 until convergence.
 *
 * Per D3: emit a `confidence` flag based on PageRank score variance.
 * Low variance = ~uniform PageRank = arbitrary "central" pick on small
 * graphs (typical of single-poem chapters).
 */

export interface TextRankResult {
  scores: number[];
  confidence: "low" | "medium" | "high";
  iterations: number;
}

export interface TextRankOpts {
  damping?: number;
  edgeThreshold?: number;
  maxIter?: number;
  convergence?: number;
  /** Variance below this = confidence "low" (D3). */
  lowVarianceThreshold?: number;
  /** Variance above this = confidence "high". Otherwise "medium". */
  highVarianceThreshold?: number;
}

export function textRank(
  similarity: number[][],
  opts: TextRankOpts = {},
): TextRankResult {
  const damping = opts.damping ?? 0.85;
  const threshold = opts.edgeThreshold ?? 0.1;
  const maxIter = opts.maxIter ?? 50;
  const convergence = opts.convergence ?? 1e-6;
  const lowVar = opts.lowVarianceThreshold ?? 0.005;
  const highVar = opts.highVarianceThreshold ?? 0.02;
  const N = similarity.length;
  if (N === 0) return { scores: [], confidence: "low", iterations: 0 };
  if (N === 1) return { scores: [1.0], confidence: "low", iterations: 0 };

  // Adjacency (zero out diagonal + sub-threshold edges)
  const adj: number[][] = similarity.map((row, i) =>
    row.map((v, j) => (i === j || v < threshold ? 0 : v)),
  );
  // Out-degree (row-sum) per node
  const outDeg = adj.map((row) => row.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0));
  let scores = Array<number>(N).fill(1 / N);
  let iter = 0;
  for (; iter < maxIter; iter++) {
    const next = Array<number>(N).fill((1 - damping) / N);
    for (let j = 0; j < N; j++) {
      for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {
        if (adj[i]![j] === 0 || outDeg[i] === 0) continue;
        next[j]! += damping * scores[i]! * (adj[i]![j]! / outDeg[i]!);
      }
    }
    let delta = 0;
    for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) delta += Math.abs(next[i]! - scores[i]!);
    scores = next;
    if (delta < convergence) {
      iter += 1;
      break;
    }
  }
  // Normalize so scores sum to 1 (helps confidence threshold tuning)
  const total = scores.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
  if (total > 0) scores = scores.map((s) => s / total);
  // Confidence from variance
  const mean = 1 / N;
  const variance = scores.reduce((s, x) => s + (x - mean) ** 2, 0) / N;
  const confidence: "low" | "medium" | "high" =
    variance < lowVar ? "low" : variance < highVar ? "medium" : "high";
  return { scores, confidence, iterations: iter };
}
```

### Task 7: LexRank cross-check (full sheet only)

**Files:**
- Create: `apps/mcp/lib/wiki/lexrank.ts`
- Test: `apps/mcp/lib/wiki/lexrank.test.ts`

- [ ] **Step 7.1: Write test, implement, verify, commit**

LexRank is structurally identical to TextRank but with cosine threshold = 0.2 (denser graph) and unweighted edges. Reuse the textrank implementation by parameterization, OR copy with the different defaults. Recommend: a thin wrapper.

`apps/mcp/lib/wiki/lexrank.ts`:

```typescript
import { textRank, type TextRankResult } from "./textrank";

/**
 * LexRank (Erkan & Radev 2004). Same PageRank trick as TextRank but with:
 *   - threshold 0.2 (denser graph)
 *   - unweighted edges (binary above-threshold)
 *
 * Used as a cross-check in the full sheet — if TextRank and LexRank
 * disagree on top-3, that's a flag.
 */
export function lexRank(similarity: number[][]): TextRankResult {
  const binarized = similarity.map((row) =>
    row.map((v) => (v >= 0.2 ? 1.0 : 0.0)),
  );
  return textRank(binarized, { edgeThreshold: 0.5 });
}
```

Test (`apps/mcp/lib/wiki/lexrank.test.ts`):

```typescript
import { describe, expect, test } from "bun:test";
import { lexRank } from "./lexrank";

describe("LexRank", () => {
  test("returns same shape as textRank", () => {
    const sim = [
      [1.0, 0.3, 0.5],
      [0.3, 1.0, 0.4],
      [0.5, 0.4, 1.0],
    ];
    const out = lexRank(sim);
    expect(out.scores).toHaveLength(3);
  });

  test("denser threshold means edges that TextRank drops are kept", () => {
    const sim = [
      [1.0, 0.15, 0.3],
      [0.15, 1.0, 0.25],
      [0.3, 0.25, 1.0],
    ];
    const out = lexRank(sim);
    // All scores positive (no isolated node)
    expect(out.scores.every((s) => s > 0)).toBe(true);
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 8.2: Implement**

`apps/mcp/lib/wiki/refrains.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * Within-chapter refrain detection. For each pair of paragraphs (i, j)
 * where j > i, compute normalized token-level edit distance. If below
 * threshold, classify as a refrain. Group transitively.
 *
 * Threshold default 0.05 (≤5% token edits) per spec. Tunable.
 *
 * Per spec Q3: cross-corpus refrain detection (different works) is
 * deferred to v2 via MinHash. Within-chapter is sufficient for v1.
 */

export interface RefrainParagraph {
  id: string;
  tokens: string[];
}

export interface RefrainResult {
  /** Verbatim phrase: the longest common token-prefix across all members. */
  phrase: string;
  count: number;
  cites: string[]; // paragraph IDs in chapter order
}

export interface RefrainOpts {
  threshold?: number;
}

/** Token-level Levenshtein, normalized by max length. */
function normalizedEditDistance(a: string[], b: string[]): number {
  const max = Math.max(a.length, b.length);
  if (max === 0) return 0;
  const dp: number[][] = Array.from({ length: a.length + 1 }, () =>
    Array(b.length + 1).fill(0),
  );
  for (let i = 0; i <= a.length; i++) dp[i]![0] = i;
  for (let j = 0; j <= b.length; j++) dp[0]![j] = j;
  for (let i = 1; i <= a.length; i++) {
    for (let j = 1; j <= b.length; j++) {
      const cost = a[i - 1] === b[j - 1] ? 0 : 1;
      dp[i]![j] = Math.min(
        dp[i - 1]![j]! + 1,
        dp[i]![j - 1]! + 1,
        dp[i - 1]![j - 1]! + cost,
      );
    }
  }
  return dp[a.length]![b.length]! / max;
}

export function detectRefrains(
  paragraphs: RefrainParagraph[],
  opts: RefrainOpts = {},
): RefrainResult[] {
  const threshold = opts.threshold ?? 0.05;
  // Union-find for transitive grouping
  const parent = paragraphs.map((_, i) => i);
  const find = (x: number): number => {
    while (parent[x] !== x) {
      parent[x] = parent[parent[x]!]!;
      x = parent[x]!;
    }
    return x;
  };
  const union = (x: number, y: number) => {
    parent[find(x)] = find(y);
  };
  for (let i = 0; i < paragraphs.length; i++) {
    for (let j = i + 1; j < paragraphs.length; j++) {
      const d = normalizedEditDistance(paragraphs[i]!.tokens, paragraphs[j]!.tokens);
      if (d <= threshold) union(i, j);
    }
  }
  // Collect groups of size ≥ 2
  const groups = new Map<number, number[]>();
  for (let i = 0; i < paragraphs.length; i++) {
    const root = find(i);
    if (!groups.has(root)) groups.set(root, []);
    groups.get(root)!.push(i);
  }
  const out: RefrainResult[] = [];
  for (const members of groups.values()) {
    if (members.length < 2) continue;
    const cites = members.map((i) => paragraphs[i]!.id);
    // phrase = longest common token-prefix across all members
    const tokenLists = members.map((i) => paragraphs[i]!.tokens);
    const minLen = Math.min(...tokenLists.map((t) => t.length));
    const prefix: string[] = [];
    for (let k = 0; k < minLen; k++) {
      const token = tokenLists[0]![k]!;
      if (tokenLists.every((t) => t[k] === token)) prefix.push(token);
      else break;
    }
    out.push({ phrase: prefix.join(" "), count: members.length, cites });
  }
  out.sort((a, b) => b.count - a.count);
  return out;
}
```

### Task 9: Stylometry (TTR, hapax, Burrows' Delta)

**Files:**
- Create: `apps/mcp/lib/wiki/stylometry.ts`
- Test: `apps/mcp/lib/wiki/stylometry.test.ts`

- [ ] **Step 9.1: Write failing test, implement, verify, commit**

`apps/mcp/lib/wiki/stylometry.ts`:

```typescript
/**
 * Stylometric primitives for the wiki layer.
 *
 *   - typeTokenRatio: distinct types / total tokens
 *   - hapaxRatio: tokens-appearing-exactly-once / distinct types
 *   - burrowsDelta: per-chapter z-score over the corpus's top-K most
 *     frequent terms; chapters with delta > 1.5σ from work mean are
 *     stylistic outliers.
 */

export function typeTokenRatio(tokens: string[]): number {
  if (tokens.length === 0) return 0;
  return new Set(tokens).size / tokens.length;
}

export function hapaxRatio(tokens: string[]): number {
  const counts = new Map<string, number>();
  for (const t of tokens) counts.set(t, (counts.get(t) ?? 0) + 1);
  if (counts.size === 0) return 0;
  let hapax = 0;
  for (const c of counts.values()) if (c === 1) hapax += 1;
  return hapax / counts.size;
}

/**
 * Burrows' Delta. For chapter `target` and a reference corpus of chapters,
 * compute per-term z-score against corpus mean+stdev; Delta = mean of
 * absolute z-scores over top-K most frequent corpus terms.
 *
 * Used in the full sheet only.
 */
export function burrowsDelta(
  target: Map<string, number>,
  corpus: Map<string, number>[],
  topK = 100,
): number {
  // Top-K by combined corpus frequency
  const corpusFreq = new Map<string, number>();
  for (const ch of corpus) {
    for (const [term, c] of ch) corpusFreq.set(term, (corpusFreq.get(term) ?? 0) + c);
  }
  const topTerms = [...corpusFreq.entries()]
    .sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1])
    .slice(0, topK)
    .map(([t]) => t);
  if (topTerms.length === 0) return 0;
  // Per-term z-scores against corpus mean+stdev
  let sumAbsZ = 0;
  let counted = 0;
  for (const term of topTerms) {
    const corpusVals = corpus.map((ch) => ch.get(term) ?? 0);
    const mean =
      corpusVals.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / Math.max(corpusVals.length, 1);
    const variance =
      corpusVals.reduce((s, v) => s + (v - mean) ** 2, 0) /
      Math.max(corpusVals.length, 1);
    const stdev = Math.sqrt(variance);
    if (stdev <= 0) continue;
    const z = ((target.get(term) ?? 0) - mean) / stdev;
    sumAbsZ += Math.abs(z);
    counted += 1;
  }
  return counted > 0 ? sumAbsZ / counted : 0;
}
```

`apps/mcp/lib/wiki/stylometry.test.ts`:

```typescript
import { describe, expect, test } from "bun:test";
import { typeTokenRatio, hapaxRatio, burrowsDelta } from "./stylometry";

describe("stylometry", () => {
  test("TTR all unique → 1.0", () => {
    expect(typeTokenRatio(["a", "b", "c"])).toBe(1.0);
  });
  test("TTR all same → 1/N", () => {
    expect(typeTokenRatio(["a", "a", "a", "a"])).toBe(0.25);
  });
  test("TTR empty → 0", () => {
    expect(typeTokenRatio([])).toBe(0);
  });
  test("hapax all unique → 1.0", () => {
    expect(hapaxRatio(["a", "b", "c"])).toBe(1.0);
  });
  test("hapax half duplicated → 0.5", () => {
    expect(hapaxRatio(["a", "a", "b", "c"])).toBeCloseTo(2 / 3, 5);
  });
  test("burrows delta of identical-to-corpus chapter → 0", () => {
    const ch = new Map([["a", 0.1], ["b", 0.2]]);
    const corpus = [new Map([["a", 0.1], ["b", 0.2]]), new Map([["a", 0.1], ["b", 0.2]])];
    expect(burrowsDelta(ch, corpus)).toBeCloseTo(0, 5);
  });
});
```

```typescript
import { describe, expect, test } from "bun:test";
import { renderChapterCard, type ChapterRenderInput } from "./render-card";

const fixture: ChapterRenderInput = {
  workTitle: "Test Work",
  chapterNumber: 1,
  layout: "verse",
  paragraphCount: 3,
  wordCount: 120,
  vocabulary: { distinctTypes: 80, ttr: 0.67, hapaxPct: 0.5 },
  trigrams: [
    { ngram: "the self existent", score: 0.9 },
    { ngram: "from his mouth", score: 0.8 },
    { ngram: "great souled bhrigu", score: 0.7 },
  ],
  textRank: {
    confidence: "high",
    paragraphs: [
      { id: "p-aaaaaa", text: "Then the divine self-existent, desiring beings of many kinds." },
    ],
  },
  opens: { id: "p-aaaaaa", text: "Then the divine self-existent, desiring beings of many kinds." },
  closes: { id: "p-cccccc", text: "Such was the first creation, by the resolve of the Self-Existent." },
  refrain: { phrase: "the great-souled", count: 2, firstCite: "p-bbbbbb" },
  nearestInCorpus: [
    { workShortName: "TestSibling", chapterNumber: 1, cosine: 0.71 },
  ],
  originalLanguageSignature: null,
};

describe("renderChapterCard", () => {
  test("emits frontmatter with textrank_confidence", () => {
    const out = renderChapterCard(fixture);
    expect(out).toMatch(/^---\ntextrank_confidence: high\n---\n/);
  });
  test("emits FULL key-passage text (no truncation per D2)", () => {
    const out = renderChapterCard(fixture);
    expect(out).toContain("[p-aaaaaa] Then the divine self-existent, desiring beings of many kinds.");
  });
  test("emits trigrams as middot-separated", () => {
    const out = renderChapterCard(fixture);
    expect(out).toContain('"the self existent" · "from his mouth" · "great souled bhrigu"');
  });
  test("emits nearest-in-corpus line", () => {
    const out = renderChapterCard(fixture);
    expect(out).toContain("TestSibling ch.1 0.71");
  });
  test("omits Refrain section when refrain is null", () => {
    const noRefrain = { ...fixture, refrain: null };
    const out = renderChapterCard(noRefrain);
    expect(out).not.toContain("## Refrain");
  });
  test("omits Original-language section when null", () => {
    const out = renderChapterCard(fixture);
    expect(out).not.toContain("## Original-language signature");
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 10.3: Implement**

`apps/mcp/lib/wiki/render-card.ts`:

```typescript
export interface NgramScore {
  ngram: string;
  score: number;
}

export interface NearestRef {
  workShortName: string;
  chapterNumber: number;
  cosine: number;
}

export interface RefrainSummary {
  phrase: string;
  count: number;
  firstCite: string;
}

export interface OriginalLanguageSignature {
  language: string;
  trigrams: string[];
}

export interface ChapterRenderInput {
  workTitle: string;
  chapterNumber: number;
  layout: string;
  paragraphCount: number;
  wordCount: number;
  vocabulary: { distinctTypes: number; ttr: number; hapaxPct: number };
  trigrams: NgramScore[];
  textRank: {
    confidence: "low" | "medium" | "high";
    paragraphs: { id: string; text: string }[];
  };
  opens: { id: string; text: string };
  closes: { id: string; text: string };
  refrain: RefrainSummary | null;
  nearestInCorpus: NearestRef[];
  originalLanguageSignature: OriginalLanguageSignature | null;
}

export function renderChapterCard(c: ChapterRenderInput): string {
  const lines: string[] = [];
  lines.push("---");
  lines.push(`textrank_confidence: ${c.textRank.confidence}`);
  lines.push("---");
  lines.push("");
  lines.push(`# ${c.workTitle} · ch.${c.chapterNumber}`);
  const ttrPct = (c.vocabulary.ttr * 100).toFixed(0);
  const hapaxPct = (c.vocabulary.hapaxPct * 100).toFixed(0);
  lines.push(
    `${c.layout} · ${c.paragraphCount}¶ · ${c.wordCount}w · vocab ${c.vocabulary.distinctTypes} (TTR ${ttrPct}%, hapax ${hapaxPct}%)`,
  );
  lines.push("");
  lines.push("## Distinctive trigrams");
  lines.push(c.trigrams.map((t) => `"${t.ngram}"`).join(" · "));
  lines.push("");
  lines.push(`## Key passage (TextRank #1)`);
  for (const p of c.textRank.paragraphs.slice(0, 1)) {
    lines.push(`> [${p.id}] ${p.text}`);
  }
  lines.push("");
  lines.push("## Opens");
  lines.push(`> [${c.opens.id}] ${c.opens.text}`);
  lines.push("");
  lines.push("## Closes");
  lines.push(`> [${c.closes.id}] ${c.closes.text}`);
  lines.push("");
  if (c.refrain) {
    lines.push("## Refrain (top)");
    lines.push(`"${c.refrain.phrase}" — ${c.refrain.count}× (first at ${c.refrain.firstCite})`);
    lines.push("");
  }
  lines.push("## Nearest in corpus");
  lines.push(
    c.nearestInCorpus
      .map((n) => `${n.workShortName} ch.${n.chapterNumber} ${n.cosine.toFixed(2)}`)
      .join(" · "),
  );
  lines.push("");
  if (c.originalLanguageSignature) {
    const sig = c.originalLanguageSignature;
    lines.push(`## Original-language signature (${sig.language}, top-3)`);
    lines.push(sig.trigrams.map((t) => `"${t}"`).join(" · "));
    lines.push("");
  }
  return lines.join("\n");
}
```

### Task 11: Work card renderer

**Files:**
- Create: `apps/mcp/lib/wiki/render-work-card.ts`
- Test: `apps/mcp/lib/wiki/render-work-card.test.ts`

- [ ] **Step 11.1–11.5: Test → implement → verify → commit (same pattern)**

The work card has: header (title + author + era + language + layout + chapter count + total words), distinctive trigrams (top-12 across all chapters), NPMI signature (top-6), chapter map (per-chapter TextRank #1 truncated to ~80 chars on punctuation), nearest works in corpus (top-4), phrases unique to this work (top-8), original-language signature when applicable.

The chapter-map line truncation: `truncatedSentence` helper that takes the TextRank #1 paragraph text and returns the first complete clause (split on `.;` first, fall back to `,` then space at ~80 chars).

```typescript
function truncatedSentence(text: string, maxLen = 80): string {
  if (text.length <= maxLen) return text;
  const punctIdx = text.indexOf(". ");
  if (punctIdx > 0 && punctIdx <= maxLen) return text.slice(0, punctIdx + 1);
  const commaIdx = text.indexOf(", ");
  if (commaIdx > 0 && commaIdx <= maxLen) return text.slice(0, commaIdx + 1) + "...";
  // Fall back to word boundary near maxLen
  const cutoff = text.lastIndexOf(" ", maxLen);
  return (cutoff > 0 ? text.slice(0, cutoff) : text.slice(0, maxLen)) + "...";
}
```

Tests cover: header line correctness; chapter map shows ALL chapters in order with truncated text; NPMI line; uniqueness phrases; conditional original-language signature.

- [ ] **Step 13.1: Write the failing integration test**

The integration test runs the full pipeline against a 2-work fixture corpus (1 verse-Sanskrit, 1 prose-French) under `apps/mcp/test/fixtures/wiki-corpus/`. Asserts:
- Wiki dirs are created
- Each chapter has `.card.md` + `.full.md`
- Each work has `_work.card.md` + `_work.full.md`
- All emitted `[p-XXXXXX]` handles resolve to real paragraphs (R1 critical)

```typescript
import { describe, expect, test } from "bun:test";
import { existsSync, readFileSync, readdirSync } from "node:fs";
import { resolve } from "node:path";
import { buildWikiAll } from "./build-wiki";

const FIXTURE = resolve(__dirname, "../apps/mcp/test/fixtures/wiki-corpus");

describe("build-wiki integration", () => {
  test("builds card + full per chapter, work card + work full per work", async () => {
    await buildWikiAll(FIXTURE);
    const works = readdirSync(`${FIXTURE}/works`);
    for (const slug of works) {
      const wikiDir = `${FIXTURE}/works/${slug}/wiki`;
      expect(existsSync(`${wikiDir}/_work.card.md`)).toBe(true);
      expect(existsSync(`${wikiDir}/_work.full.md`)).toBe(true);
    }
  });

  test("R1: every emitted [p-XXXXXX] handle exists in source paragraphs.json", async () => {
    await buildWikiAll(FIXTURE);
    const works = readdirSync(`${FIXTURE}/works`);
    const allEmitted: string[] = [];
    for (const slug of works) {
      const wikiDir = `${FIXTURE}/works/${slug}/wiki`;
      for (const f of readdirSync(wikiDir)) {
        const content = readFileSync(`${wikiDir}/${f}`, "utf-8");
        const matches = content.matchAll(/\[(p-[0-9a-f]{6})\]/g);
        for (const m of matches) allEmitted.push(`${slug}::${m[1]}`);
      }
    }
    // Load every paragraphs.json under fixture, build set of valid hashes
    const valid = new Set<string>();
    for (const slug of works) {
      const chDir = `${FIXTURE}/works/${slug}/chapters`;
      if (!existsSync(chDir)) continue;
      for (const ch of readdirSync(chDir)) {
        const path = `${chDir}/${ch}/translation.paragraphs.json`;
        if (!existsSync(path)) continue;
        const j = JSON.parse(readFileSync(path, "utf-8"));
        for (const p of j.paragraphs ?? []) valid.add(`${slug}::${p.id}`);
      }
    }
    const orphaned = allEmitted.filter((e) => !valid.has(e));
    expect(orphaned).toEqual([]);
  });

  test("idempotent: rebuilding produces byte-identical output (R2)", async () => {
    await buildWikiAll(FIXTURE);
    const before: Record<string, string> = {};
    const works = readdirSync(`${FIXTURE}/works`);
    for (const slug of works) {
      const wikiDir = `${FIXTURE}/works/${slug}/wiki`;
      for (const f of readdirSync(wikiDir)) {
        before[`${slug}/${f}`] = readFileSync(`${wikiDir}/${f}`, "utf-8");
      }
    }
    await buildWikiAll(FIXTURE);
    for (const [key, content] of Object.entries(before)) {
      const [slug, f] = key.split("/");
      const after = readFileSync(`${FIXTURE}/works/${slug}/wiki/${f}`, "utf-8");
      expect(after).toBe(content);
    }
  });
});
```

- [ ] **Step 13.2: Implement orchestrator**

`scripts/build-wiki.ts` (skeleton):

```typescript
#!/usr/bin/env bun
/**
 * Build the wiki layer for the entire corpus or a single work.
 *
 * Modes:
 *   --check         dry-run; build to memory, diff vs on-disk, exit 1 if differ
 *   --work <slug>   single-work rebuild
 *   (default)       full corpus rebuild
 */

import { readFileSync, readdirSync, existsSync, writeFileSync, mkdirSync, renameSync, rmSync } from "node:fs";
import { resolve, join, dirname } from "node:path";
import { tokenize, type Language } from "../apps/mcp/lib/wiki/tokenize";
import { buildTfIdf, cosine } from "../apps/mcp/lib/tfidf";
import { ngrams } from "../apps/mcp/lib/wiki/ngrams";
import { computeNPMI } from "../apps/mcp/lib/wiki/npmi";
import { textRank } from "../apps/mcp/lib/wiki/textrank";
import { lexRank } from "../apps/mcp/lib/wiki/lexrank";
import { detectRefrains } from "../apps/mcp/lib/wiki/refrains";
import { typeTokenRatio, hapaxRatio, burrowsDelta } from "../apps/mcp/lib/wiki/stylometry";
import { renderChapterCard } from "../apps/mcp/lib/wiki/render-card";
import { renderWorkCard } from "../apps/mcp/lib/wiki/render-work-card";
import { renderChapterFull } from "../apps/mcp/lib/wiki/render-full";
import { renderWorkFull } from "../apps/mcp/lib/wiki/render-work-full";

interface CliArgs {
  check: boolean;
  work?: string;
  corpus: string;
}

function parseArgs(): CliArgs {
  const argv = process.argv.slice(2);
  const out: CliArgs = { check: false, corpus: resolve(import.meta.dir, "../corpus") };
  for (let i = 0; i < argv.length; i++) {
    const a = argv[i]!;
    if (a === "--check") out.check = true;
    else if (a === "--work") out.work = argv[++i];
    else if (a === "--corpus") out.corpus = argv[++i] ?? out.corpus;
  }
  return out;
}

export async function buildWikiAll(corpusRoot: string, opts: { check?: boolean; onlyWork?: string } = {}): Promise<{ wrote: number; checked: number; drift: string[] }> {
  const manifest = JSON.parse(readFileSync(`${corpusRoot}/manifest.json`, "utf-8"));
  const works = manifest.works.filter((w: any) => !opts.onlyWork || w.slug === opts.onlyWork);
  // 1. Load all chapters across all works
  // 2. Tokenize per-language
  // 3. Build corpus-level TF-IDF (unigrams)
  // 4. Build n-gram TF-IDF (bi/tri)
  // 5. For each chapter:
  //      compute paragraph-level similarity matrix
  //      run TextRank + LexRank
  //      detect refrains
  //      compute vocabulary stats
  //      run NPMI
  //      pull nearest chapters from corpus-level cosine
  //      render card + full
  // 6. For each work:
  //      aggregate work-level distinctive trigrams + NPMI
  //      build chapter map (per-chapter TextRank #1, truncated)
  //      render work card + work full
  // 7. Atomic write to corpus/works/<slug>/wiki/<file>.md
  //      .tmp + rename per file
  // 8. If --check: diff against on-disk, return drift list
  // ... (full implementation: ~300 lines)
  // return { wrote, checked, drift };
}

if (import.meta.main) {
  const args = parseArgs();
  const result = await buildWikiAll(args.corpus, { check: args.check, onlyWork: args.work });
  if (args.check && result.drift.length > 0) {
    console.error("Wiki is stale. Drifted files:");
    for (const f of result.drift) console.error(`  ${f}`);
    process.exit(1);
  }
  console.log(`wrote: ${result.wrote} files`);
}
```

The full implementation (~300 lines) is left for the implementation subagent — the orchestration logic is straightforward given the primitives are tested. The key thing is each step is exercised in the integration test.

### Task 14: Performance bench (`scripts/bench-wiki.ts`)

**Files:**

- [ ] **Step 14.1: Implement**

```typescript
#!/usr/bin/env bun
import { resolve } from "node:path";
import { buildWikiAll } from "./build-wiki";

const BUDGET_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000; // D7

async function main() {
  const corpusRoot = resolve(import.meta.dir, "../corpus");
  const start = performance.now();
  const result = await buildWikiAll(corpusRoot);
  const elapsed = performance.now() - start;
  const elapsedSec = (elapsed / 1000).toFixed(1);
  console.log(`build-wiki full corpus rebuild: ${elapsedSec}s · ${result.wrote} files`);
  if (elapsed > BUDGET_MS) {
    console.error(`PERF BUDGET EXCEEDED: ${elapsedSec}s > 300s`);
    process.exit(1);
  }
}

main();
```

- [ ] **Step 15.2: Implement in tools.ts**

```typescript
export function read_wiki(corpus: Corpus, work_slug: string, chapter_number?: number): { markdown: string } {
  // Validate work exists
  if (!corpus.work(work_slug)) {
    throw new MCPError(`Unknown work slug: ${work_slug}`, "UNKNOWN_WORK");
  }
  const wikiDir = `${corpus.workDir(work_slug)}/wiki`;
  if (!existsSync(wikiDir)) {
    throw new MCPError(`Wiki not built for ${work_slug}. Run: bun run scripts/build-wiki.ts --work ${work_slug}`, "WIKI_NOT_BUILT");
  }
  if (chapter_number === undefined) {
    return { markdown: readFileSync(`${wikiDir}/_work.card.md`, "utf-8") };
  }
  // Find the chapter dir matching chapter_number, then read <chapter-slug>.card.md
  const chapter = corpus.chapter(work_slug, chapter_number);
  if (!chapter) {
    throw new MCPError(`Chapter ${chapter_number} not found in ${work_slug}`, "UNKNOWN_CHAPTER");
  }
  const cardPath = `${wikiDir}/${chapter.chapter_slug}.card.md`;
  if (!existsSync(cardPath)) {
    throw new MCPError(`Wiki card missing for ${work_slug}/${chapter.chapter_slug}`, "WIKI_MISSING_CARD");
  }
  return { markdown: readFileSync(cardPath, "utf-8") };
}

export function read_wiki_full(corpus: Corpus, work_slug: string, chapter_number?: number): { markdown: string } {
  // Same shape as read_wiki but reads .full.md
  // ... (same implementation, swap .card.md → .full.md)
}
```

```typescript
import { describe, expect, test } from "bun:test";
import { Corpus } from "../src/corpus";
import { list_works, read_wiki, read_chapter } from "../src/tools";

const corpus = new Corpus();

describe("LLM navigation flow (E2E)", () => {
  test("list_works → read_wiki(slug) → read_wiki(slug, ch) → read_chapter → quote", () => {
    // Step 1: pick a work
    const works = list_works(corpus);
    expect(works.count).toBeGreaterThan(0);
    const targetSlug = works.works[0]!.slug;

    // Step 2: read the work card
    const workCard = read_wiki(corpus, targetSlug);
    expect(workCard.markdown).toContain("## Chapter map");

    // Step 3: read a specific chapter card
    const chapterCard = read_wiki(corpus, targetSlug, 1);
    expect(chapterCard.markdown).toMatch(/\[p-[0-9a-f]{6}\]/);

    // Step 4: extract a [p-XXXXXX] handle from the card
    const handleMatch = chapterCard.markdown.match(/\[(p-[0-9a-f]{6})\]/);
    expect(handleMatch).toBeDefined();
    const handle = handleMatch![1]!;

    // Step 5: drill into the full chapter
    const chapter = read_chapter(corpus, targetSlug, 1);
    expect(chapter.body).toContain(handle);
  });
});
```

```typescript
import { describe, expect, test } from "bun:test";
import { existsSync, readdirSync, readFileSync } from "node:fs";
import { resolve } from "node:path";

const CORPUS_ROOT = resolve(__dirname, "../../../corpus");
const works = readdirSync(`${CORPUS_ROOT}/works`);

describe("R1 (CRITICAL): paragraph-hash existence across wiki output", () => {
  for (const slug of works) {
    const wikiDir = `${CORPUS_ROOT}/works/${slug}/wiki`;
    if (!existsSync(wikiDir)) continue; // wiki not built for this work — separate failure mode

    test(`${slug}: every [p-XXXXXX] handle resolves to a real paragraph`, () => {
      const valid = new Set<string>();
      const chDir = `${CORPUS_ROOT}/works/${slug}/chapters`;
      for (const ch of readdirSync(chDir)) {
        const path = `${chDir}/${ch}/translation.paragraphs.json`;
        if (!existsSync(path)) continue;
        const j = JSON.parse(readFileSync(path, "utf-8"));
        for (const p of j.paragraphs ?? []) valid.add(p.id);
      }
      const orphaned: string[] = [];
      for (const f of readdirSync(wikiDir)) {
        const content = readFileSync(`${wikiDir}/${f}`, "utf-8");
        const matches = content.matchAll(/\[(p-[0-9a-f]{6})\]/g);
        for (const m of matches) {
          if (!valid.has(m[1]!)) orphaned.push(`${f}: ${m[1]}`);
        }
      }
      expect(orphaned).toEqual([]);
    });
  }
});
```

- [ ] **Step 18.1: Implement workflow**

```yaml
name: wiki-ci

on:
  pull_request:
    paths:
      - "corpus/**"
      - "apps/mcp/lib/**"
      - "scripts/build-wiki.ts"
      - "scripts/bench-wiki.ts"

jobs:
  staleness-and-bench:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v1
        with:
          bun-version: latest
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: bun install
      - name: Wiki staleness check (D4)
        run: bun run scripts/build-wiki.ts --check
      - name: Wiki perf bench (D7)
        run: bun run scripts/bench-wiki.ts
      - name: Wiki R1 paragraph-hash existence
        run: bun test apps/mcp/test/wiki-r1-paragraph-hash-existence.test.ts
      - name: Wiki full unit suite
```

## Implementation summary

After all 21 tasks: 

- 11 new modules under `apps/mcp/lib/` and `apps/mcp/lib/wiki/` (shared TF-IDF + 7 statistical primitives + 4 renderers)
- 1 refactored existing module (`scripts/cross-link.ts` imports from shared lib)
- 2 new scripts (`scripts/build-wiki.ts` orchestrator + `scripts/bench-wiki.ts` perf gate)
- 2 new MCP tools (`read_wiki`, `read_wiki_full`)
- 1 new GitHub Actions workflow (staleness gate + bench + R1)
- ~800 generated wiki files committed under `corpus/works/*/wiki/`
- ~25 test files (per-primitive units + integration + E2E + R1 critical)

All locked decisions from the engineering review (D1–D7) and the IRON RULE (R1) implemented and tested.
